articles.title: Trump rejects Iran response, calls it 'inappropriate'
articles.summary: Trump rejects Iran response, calls it 'inappropriate' US President Donald Trump has sharpened his criticism of Iran’s response to a US ceasefire proposal, reiterating his rejection of the document. Speaking to Axios, Trump said: “I don’t like their letter. It’s inappropriate,” declining to give further details. He also said he had a “very nice call” with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, but stressed that negotiations with Iran are “my situation, not everybody else’s”.

articles.summary: Netanyahu says Iran war 'not over' amid fragile US ceasefire Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has warned that the war with Iran is “not over”, despite a fragile US-brokered ceasefire. "Now, we've degraded a lot of it," Netanyahu said, referring to Iran’s nuclear capabilities, its allied forces across the region and its missile production. And a venture investor says the smartest money isn't on any one brand, it's on the companies that supply them all. (Source: Bloomberg)
